title: allium stephanophorum
intro: Allium stephanophorum, commonly known as the white-flowered garlic, is a species of plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. It is native to the Himalayan region and is used for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiviral activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Herbal preparations of Allium stephanophorum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
